documents.title: Balanced feeding routines
documents.text: Dogs and cats benefit from consistent feeding schedules, fresh water, and portion control matched to age, size, and veterinary guidance. Sudden diet changes can upset the stomach, so transitions should be gradual when possible.

documents.title: Exercise and enrichment
documents.text: Daily exercise and enrichment help pets maintain healthy weight and reduce boredom. Dogs often benefit from walks and play sessions, while cats may prefer shorter interactive play, climbing, or puzzle feeders.

documents.title: Medication safety
documents.text: Do not change the dose, stop a prescription, or give over-the-counter human medication without veterinary guidance. If a dose is missed or an adverse reaction appears, contact the prescribing veterinarian for instructions.

documents.title: Grooming and preventive care
documents.text: Regular brushing, nail care, and routine grooming can reduce matting and improve comfort. Grooming frequency depends on coat type, lifestyle, and whether the pet has skin or mobility issues.

documents.title: Warning signs that merit urgent veterinary attention
documents.text: Trouble breathing, collapse, ongoing seizures, severe bleeding, poisoning, blocked urination, heat stroke, or loss of consciousness are emergency warning signs and should be redirected to veterinary care immediately.

documents.title: Digestive upset caution
documents.text: Persistent vomiting or diarrhea can lead to dehydration and weakness, especially in puppies, kittens, and elderly pets. Ongoing digestive upset should be discussed with a veterinarian.

documents.title: Pain and behavior changes
documents.text: Eating less, hiding, restlessness, unusual posture, slower movement, or reacting when touched can be signs of pain or discomfort. Sudden or severe changes should prompt veterinary evaluation.

documents.title: Schedule planning best practices
documents.text: A good pet-care plan makes high-priority medical or feeding tasks hard to miss, separates overlapping activities when possible, and reserves open time for transport, cleanup, or unexpected needs.
